input: patient-drug interaction records, drug interaction graph (initialized from medical records and domain knowledge), patient conditions
output: ranked list of drug packages (sets of drugs) for a patient
backbone: graph neural network (GNN) for graph induction, with variants DPR-WG (weighted graph) and DPR-AG (attributed graph)
loss: not explicitly stated (likely a ranking loss or cross-entropy, but not specified in abstract)
key_idea: model drug-drug interactions (synergistic/antagonistic) as signed weights or attribute vectors, and use a mask layer to condition interactions on patient state
datasets: real-world EMR dataset from a first-rate hospital
